name: witcherscript
description: WitcherScript language support, with code completion, diagnostics, and more.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Spawns a native language-server binary and reads workspace .ws files; not safe in untrusted workspaces.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Requires local filesystem access for the bundled language server and the Witcher 3 install.

scripts.check: npm run gen:meta && tsc --noEmit && node --check scripts/prepare-server.mjs && node --check esbuild.mjs
scripts.package: npm run prepare:server && vsce package --target win32-x64
scripts.prerelease: npm run prepare:server && vsce package --pre-release --target win32-x64

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with WitcherScript
walkthroughs.description: Set up language features for Witcher 3 modding.

steps.title: Welcome
steps.description: Language features for `.ws` files: diagnostics, hover, navigation, and formatting via a bundled language server.

steps.title: Game directory detected
steps.description: Your Witcher 3 install was detected automatically. Nothing to do here — open `Settings` if you want to verify or override the path.

steps.title: Set your Witcher 3 game directory
steps.description:
The language server needs your Witcher 3 install to resolve base game scripts. On Windows with a GOG install this is detected automatically — if not, use the button below.

[Select Game Folder](command:witcherscript.setGameDirectory)

steps.title: Add scripts from other mods
steps.description:
Reads `.ws` files from another mod - shared utilities, frameworks, anything you depend on. For modern mods that *add* new scripts.

[Add Folder](command:witcherscript.addAdditionalScriptDirectory)

steps.title: Add legacy script overrides
steps.description:
Optional: For older mods that have edited copies of base game scripts. The server uses your copy instead of the original.

[Add Legacy Mod](command:witcherscript.addLegacyScriptDirectory)

steps.title: Open a WitcherScript file
steps.description: Open any `.ws` file to get diagnostics, hover documentation, Go to Definition, and document symbols.

witcherscript.gameDirectory.description: Absolute path to The Witcher 3 game directory (e.g. C:\games\The Witcher 3 Wild Hunt GOTY). Used by the language server to locate base game scripts.

witcherscript.autoLoadModSharedImports.description: Auto-load the Shared Imports mod from <gameDirectory>\Mods\modSharedImports if present. Most modern Witcher 3 mods depend on it. Disable to opt out.

witcherscript.logLevel.description: Log verbosity for the WitcherScript language server. Messages appear in the WitcherScript output channel.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Diagnose every `.ws` file in the project on startup. The Problems list shows diagnostics for all workspace files.
Diagnose only the files currently open in the editor. Symbols are still indexed project-wide so go-to-definition and completion work everywhere, but the heavy whole-project checking is skipped.

witcherscript.codeLens.overriddenSymbols.markdownDescription: Show a code lens above each top-level symbol in a legacy override script that shadows a base game symbol, linking to the base game definition.

witcherscript.codeLens.references.markdownDescription: Show a reference-count code lens above each top-level declaration and class method.

witcherscript.additionalScriptDirectorie.markdownDescription:
Folders to scan for extra `.ws` script files, subfolders included. If your scripts use functions from other mods, you can add them here (e.g. Shared Utils).

See also `#witcherscript.legacyScriptDirectories#`.

[Add folder (Workspace)…](command:witcherscript.addAdditionalScriptDirectory)

witcherscript.legacyScriptDirectories.markdownDescription:
Folders to scan for extra `.ws` script files, subfolders included. A script found here **replaces** the game's built-in script of the same name, instead of clashing with it. Use this for older mods that overwrite the game's own script files.

For mods that add *new* scripts alongside the game's own, use `#witcherscript.additionalScriptDirectories#` instead.

[Add folder (Workspace)…](command:witcherscript.addLegacyScriptDirectory)

witcherscript.formatter.lineLimit.description: Maximum line length the formatter will target before wrapping.

witcherscript.formatter.compactColon.description: When enabled, the formatter omits spaces around the colon in type annotations (e.g. `var x:int` instead of `var x : int`).

witcherscript.formatter.alignMemberColon.description: When enabled, the formatter vertically aligns the colons of consecutive class, struct, and state field declarations into a column. A blank line, comment, or non-field member breaks a group.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Keep same-line vs split as in source; normalise whitespace only.
Annotation and member always on one line.
witcherscript.formatter.annotationPlacem.markdownDescription: How the formatter places annotations relative to their member.

witcherscript.server.path.description: Optional path to the witcherscript-lsp executable. If empty, the extension uses the bundled server binary.

witcherscript.server.tcpPort.markdownDescription: **LSP Testing:** Connect to an externally running language server on this localhost TCP port instead of launching the bundled binary. Leave at `0` to use the bundled server.
